title: VCP Capability Manifest
description: A signed, immutable document fully describing one VCP capability. See SPECIFICATION.md §5.2. Identity (capability.id / contract_hash) is sha256(JCS(contract)) where `contract` is the security-relevant subset defined in §4: name, version, input_schema, output_schema, effects, determinism, sandbox, and issuer. summary_for_user, summary_for_model, provenance, and signature are NOT part of the contract.

issuer.description: DID or URI identifying the signing provider, e.g. did:web:example.com. Part of the contract.

id.description: Content-addressed identity: vcp:cap:<name>@sha256:<hex> where hex = contract_hash digest.

contract_hash.description: sha256(JCS(contract)). MUST match the digest embedded in `id`.

summary_for_user.description: Display string shown to the user at approval time. NOT part of the contract; NOT an instruction.

summary_for_model.description: Display string the Gateway MAY compile into an affordance for the Planner. NOT part of the contract; NEVER passed verbatim as instruction.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema for arguments. Every object level MUST set additionalProperties:false (schema-confusion / hidden-argument defense, §17).

requires_attestation.description: When true, the Gateway MUST verify the Provider's environment attestation (§27) before minting a grant. Off by default to avoid friction.

attestation_tier.description: Minimum environment-attestation tier required (§27.3). Default statement.

compensating_action.description: REQUIRED when class is write-reversible: the capability that undoes a committed effect (§11).

filesystem.description: Default none. Anything else is an explicit allowlist (§14).

network.description: Network egress allowlist. Empty array = deny all (§14).

secrets.description: Named secrets injected by the Gateway secret broker. Never raw env vars (§14).

kind.description: tool | resource | prompt | workflow | state | event | task | interface (§5.1).

interface.description: Present for kind=interface (§22). The UI artifact is content-addressed and signed; the Host MUST verify content_hash and render sandboxed under the declared CSP.

render.description: Render mode; html-sandboxed = isolated iframe, no ambient origin.

csp.description: Content-Security-Policy directives. Absent ⇒ Host MUST apply deny-all default.

host_actions.description: The only capabilities this UI may invoke; each call re-enters the full grant pipeline.

model_visible.description: false ⇒ UI-only control hidden from the Planner.

command.description: Present for kind=command (§28). A content-addressed, argv-typed CLI invocation. NEVER executed via a shell; the Gateway exec's `binary` with the resolved argv array.

exec_digest.description: Pinned hash of the resolved executable (§28.4). A changed binary is a new identity.

shell.description: MUST be false. VCP never passes commands to a shell (§28.1).

argv_template.description: Ordered argv tokens. A literal token is a string; a typed hole is {param, schema}. Each param value occupies exactly one argv element and is never re-split or shell-expanded.

schema.description: JSON Schema for this parameter's value; SHOULD constrain paths/hosts to the sandbox scope.

working_dir.description: Working directory; MUST be within sandbox.filesystem allowlist.

provenance.description: host_cli marks a bridged existing CLI (§28.4).

subcommand_allow.description: For bridged CLIs: the allowed subcommand/flag patterns, as a signed contract rather than host-local settings.

provenance.description: Build/source provenance. NOT part of the contract. See RFC 0002.
